name: Meloidae
diagnosis: Blister beetles are beetles of the family Meloidae, so called for their defensive secretion of a blistering agent, cantharidin. About 7,500 species are known worldwide. Many are conspicuous and some are aposematically colored, announcing their toxicity to would-be predators.

genus_notes.lytta: Lytta is a beetle genus in the family Meloidae.
genus_notes.epicauta: Epicauta is a genus of beetles in the blister beetle family, Meloidae.
genus_notes.meloe: The blister beetle genus Meloe is a large, widespread group commonly referred to as oil beetles.
genus_notes.tricrania: Tricrania is a genus of blister beetles in the family Meloidae.
genus_notes.rhyphonemognatha: Rhyphonemognatha is a genus of blister beetles in the family Meloidae.
